title: Macroscepis tristis
intro: Macroscepis tristis, also known as the Brazilian angel's trumpet, is a plant species in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the tropical forests of Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Macroscepis tristis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Macroscepis tristis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and cold.
